patches.find: Lynch 2012, Irish Math. Soc. Bulletin 70; Holbrow et al. 2013, arXiv:1302.7179
patches.replace: Lynch 2012, Irish Math. Soc. Bulletin 69; Holbrow 2013, arXiv:1302.0765
patches.rationale: Two citation errors: (1) Lynch's analemma paper is Bulletin 69 (Summer 2012, pp.47-56), not Bulletin 70 (which has a Mirimanoff polynomial paper); (2) arXiv:1302.7179 is a condensed-matter physics paper on Cr-doped TiO2 by Da Pieve et al., not an analemma paper. Correct identifier is arXiv:1302.0765 (Holbrow, 'Build your own analemma'). Holbrow is single-author, not 'et al.'

patches.find: lobe height ratio ≈ 2.25, lobe width ratio ≈ 3.01, and lobe area ratio ≈ 6.48 — none equal 2.66
patches.replace: lobe height ratio ≈ 2.25, lobe width ratio ≈ 3.01, lobe area ratio ≈ 6.48, and lobe perimeter ratio ≈ 2.56 — none equal 2.66
patches.rationale: Add perimeter ratio to close the loophole: the curmudgeon computed perimeter ratio ≈ 2.56, which is 4% away from the dome's 2.66 at the two-significant-figure precision the dome quotes. All four measurable geometric ratios differ from 2.66, strengthening the argument.

patches.find: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable
patches.replace: for WIN-020 the weaker timestamp test is inapplicable in the opposite direction — there is no monitor.py handler, no hardcoded expected value, and no live fetch; the period appears only as a metadata label
patches.rationale: WIN-020 has code_analysis.monitoring='none' — there is no live fetch and no hardcoded value. Applying the 'same commit as fetch' language is an internal inconsistency (it describes monitoring='hardcoded' WINs). The corrected text accurately describes WIN-020's actual structural posture.

patches.find: The numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters (disc radius, firmament height, chosen symmetry axis, or similar), and the "confirmation" consists of matching that derivation to a globe-measured value — which, because the dome parameters were themselves fit to globe data (see Kill-Shot #1 and Part 2b), is circular. There is no pre-registration of the derivation in a venue that timestamps independently of the author, and the dome monitor script (where one exists) encodes the expected value in the same commit as the comparison.
patches.replace: θ = −lonE is not derived from dome geometry — it is a pure coordinate convention that follows from the definition of geographic longitude. There is no derivation from dome parameters (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric wave speed) to evaluate; the "prediction" consists of naming a convention the dome was already using. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is definitional circularity, not retrodiction: the identity is vacuously true for any polar-azimuthal projection centered on the North Pole.
patches.rationale: detail_evidence explicitly states 'θ = −lonE is not derived from dome geometry. It is derived from the definition of longitude. No dome-specific physics enters the derivation.' The old verdict paragraph said the opposite ('numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters'), creating a direct self-contradiction. Replace with WIN-058-specific language that matches the evidence.

patches.find: V13 merely dropped V12's unnecessary solar-noon-UTC indirection.
patches.replace: V13 promotes geographic longitude directly to θ, cleaning up V9's solar-noon-UTC proxy — but V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so two incompatible angular conventions now coexist without reconciliation.
patches.rationale: Two errors fixed: (1) The solar-noon-UTC proxy was V9-era (not V12's) — V12 introduced the 0.9941 EW scale factor; (2) the claim that V12's indirection was 'dropped' is false — the 0.9941 factor from V12 is still live in distance_analysis.py (delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941). The revised finding matches both the detail_evidence and the actual code state.

patches.replace: an ≈176° error (minimum angular distance on the circle between +32.5° and −151.2°)
patches.rationale: The 183° figure uses linear arithmetic on signed angles. The correct minimum angular distance on the circle is min(183.7°, 360° − 183.7°) = 176.3°. The qualitative argument (Sydney placed in the wrong quadrant) is unchanged.

patches.find: Pattern — acknowledge-without-fix, three instances in 24 hours.
patches.replace: Pattern — acknowledge-without-fix, three instances in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17).
patches.rationale: Per poller change files chg-20260417-1620-001 and -004, all three acknowledgements were shipped in a single commit (6e06efb) on 2026-04-17, not independently over 24 hours. A single commit with three documentary-only acknowledgements is actually a stronger data point than three spread-out instances; the correction strengthens the argument while removing the factual error.

patches.find: Three acknowledgements inside a single 24-hour window, zero structural fixes to the user-facing site.
patches.replace: Three acknowledgements in a single commit, zero structural fixes to the user-facing site.
patches.rationale: Companion to ISS-1139 patch: remove the '24-hour' framing from the evidence paragraph that follows the header, for consistency.
